bottleneck.description: convertTensor_kernel fires twice per conv call (FP32->TF32 layout coercion), totalling 60 extra kernel launches and 222us (12.8% of all cudnn_conv wall time). Both kernels show 0% tensor core activity. They exist because cuDNN HMMA path requires NHWC TF32 layout; FP32 NCHW inputs trigger a layout/dtype cast before dispatch.

transformation.description: Insert a cast to torch.float16 or torch.bfloat16 at graph input before the first placeholder->conv edge. With FP16/BF16 inputs, cuDNN selects the HMMA kernel directly, skipping both convertTensor_kernel invocations.
fx_steps: Intercept placeholder/get_attr nodes feeding into aten::cudnn_convolution
Insert call_function node for aten.to.dtype(torch.float16) after each placeholder
Run dtype propagation pass to ensure downstream BN running stats remain FP32
Verify no FP32 accumulation regression in BN/addmm nodes

bottleneck.description: cuDNN implicit GEMM kernel runs at 8.3% warp occupancy due to 150 registers/thread. At 150 regs/warp, each SM can schedule at most floor(max_regs_per_SM / (128_threads * 150_regs)) = 1 warp block. Grid of 512-1024 blocks leaves most SMs idle. Tensor core activity reaches 67-73% within active SMs but SM throughput is only 54-60% because latency cannot be hidden with so few in-flight warps.

transformation.description: Enable cudnn.benchmark to allow cuDNN to select a lower-register-count algorithm for these tile shapes. If algorithm remains high-register, replace these FX nodes with aten.convolution dispatched through Triton via torch.compile + max_autotune=True for occupancy-aware block shapes. Increasing batch size to 32+ raises grid to 1024-2048 and fills the wave pipeline.
fx_steps: Set torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark = True before torch.compile
If Kernel still dispatches with 150 regs, register FX substitution pass replacing these two aten::cudnn_convolution nodes
Replace with aten.convolution + torch._inductor.config.max_autotune = True
Alternatively, increase batch size to saturate SM wave count
transformation.assumption: GPU has >=64 SMs based on observed sm_throughput behavior

impact.note: Wide estimate range; re-profile after cudnn.benchmark to narrow

operators: aten::batch_norm (single FX node, 7 Triton kernels per iteration, 70 total)

bottleneck.description: Inductor decomposed inference-mode BN (_native_batch_norm_legit_no_training) into 7 Triton kernels including two redundant reduction passes (triton_red and triton_per) that re-read the activation tensor to accumulate channel means. At inference with frozen running_mean/running_var, these reductions are unnecessary. DRAM throughput at 67.6% confirms bandwidth-bound execution from extra passes.

transformation.description: Match aten.batch_norm(training=False) in FX graph and decompose in-place to a single aten.mul + aten.add using pre-computed scale and bias_eff constants. This produces a single elementwise x*scale+bias_eff node that inductor fuses with adjacent relu into one triton_poi kernel, eliminating the two reduction passes and all split tile kernels.
fx_steps: Symbolic trace model and locate aten.batch_norm nodes where training=False
Compute scale = weight / sqrt(running_var + eps) as get_attr constant
Compute bias_eff = bias - running_mean * scale as get_attr constant
Replace batch_norm node with call_function(aten.add, call_function(aten.mul, x, scale), bias_eff)
Allow inductor to re-fuse the resulting aten.mul -> aten.add -> aten.relu chain

operators: aten::conv2d (1 FX node, triton_poi_fused_convolution_0 and triton_poi_fused_convolution_1)

bottleneck.description: The aten::conv2d node emits two standalone Triton elementwise kernels per iteration: a bias-add scatter onto the conv output (convolution_0, grid 128x2x1) and a weight normalization read on the 3-channel weight tensor (convolution_1, grid 1x3x1). The convolution_1 kernel reads only ~12 bytes from DRAM (3-channel bias scalar), meaning kernel launch latency (~900ns) completely dominates execution time. Both kernels show 0% tensor core and <10% occupancy. Inductor did not fold these bias-add ops.

transformation.description: Detect the aten::conv2d(bias=True) -> aten::batch_norm subgraph in the FX graph. Zero conv.bias and absorb it into bn.bias via: bn.bias += conv.bias_orig * bn.weight / sqrt(bn.running_var + eps). This is a constant folding on the get_attr nodes that is semantics-preserving under training=False. Eliminates both triton_poi_fused_convolution_* kernels entirely.
fx_steps: Match subgraph: aten.convolution(bias!=None) -> aten.batch_norm(training=False)
Extract conv_bias from get_attr node
Compute absorbed_bias = conv_bias * bn_weight / sqrt(bn_running_var + eps)
Add absorbed_bias to bn_bias get_attr constant in-place
Replace conv bias with zeros (or set bias=False in aten.convolution node)

impact.note: Small absolute gain but removes degenerate 12-byte DRAM read kernel

operators: aten::addmm (10 FX nodes, each dispatching gemmSN_TN_kernel)

bottleneck.description: gemmSN_TN_kernel is the cuBLAS serial-N GEMM path, selected when output matrix dimension N is below the tensor-core tiling threshold. Grid is [2,2,1] = 4 thread blocks, SM throughput 0.2%, 0% tensor core activity, achieved occupancy 8.2%. 4 active thread blocks cannot hide memory latency on a high-SM GPU — each call is wave-starved. The _TN suffix indicates transpose-N layout (row-major A, column-major B).

transformation.description: Inspect aten::addmm node weight tensor shapes in the FX graph. If any dimension is not a multiple of 16 (FP16) or 8 (BF16), add a graph pass to pad the weight matrix to the next multiple-of-16 boundary and slice the output post-GEMM. If the 10 addmm ops are independent (no data dependency chain), batch them into a single aten::bmm with stacked inputs along batch dim to give cuBLAS a larger effective tile and promote tensor core dispatch.
fx_steps: Inspect weight shape for each aten::addmm node
If dim not multiple of 16: insert aten.constant_pad_nd before addmm, aten.slice after
Check for independent addmm nodes (topological sort to verify no inter-dependencies)
If independent: stack weight get_attr nodes, replace with single aten::bmm
Alternatively: wrap with torch.compile + torch.nn.functional.linear for Triton GEMM dispatch
transformation.assumption: Actual matrix dimensions not logged in profile; shapes must be inspected at FX trace time

impact.note: Low absolute impact at current 1.4% share; becomes more relevant after OPT-1/3 reduce total time
